start.line: So you owe Nib nine dollars, and instead of paying Nib nine dollars, you're going to spend all day acquiring nine dollars from elsewhere. That's not a plan, Artie. That's a lateral move with extra steps.

options.text: It's about the PRINCIPLE. I already paid.
Where would you even get nine dollars?
You're supposed to be on my side.
I don't have time for material, Sol.

principle.line: It's never about the principle. It's about the nine dollars. It's ALWAYS about the nine dollars. That's the whole show.

hints.line: You've got a jacket you hate — that store took my toaster back, they'll take your crime against plaid. And there's Trivia Night, if you can get one of your walking encyclopedias to stand still near a buzzer. Two schemes, one tab. Go.

options.text: The jacket and the trivia. Got it.

side.line: I'm on the side of watching this happen. That's the best seat in the house and it's the seat I bought.
